mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS declines exceed 50k.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS/HOUST and SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply chain capacity, creating inflationary pressures that push S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines exceed 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% triggers central bank tightening expectations, reducing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1) indicates deteriorating economic fundamentals, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 9% signals weakening labor market conditions that lower consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates overheating supply-side capacity, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved earnings visibility.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO/UNRATE and SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
